name: Participant Recruitment Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Increased participant pool → Systemic: Enhanced data quality and statistical power → Strategic: Improved research outcomes and grant prospects.
options: Recruit through sleep clinics only
Expand to regional neurologist referrals and online forums
Incorporate social media campaigns and targeted advertising
review: Controls participant recruitment vs. research ethics. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on participant privacy and data protection.

name: Sensor Suite Configuration
consequences: Immediate: Improved sensor coverage → Systemic: Enhanced data resolution and accuracy → Strategic: Increased research opportunities and publication prospects.
options: Standard PSG montage with dry-electrode EEG headbands
Enhanced nights with fuller PSG montage including scalp EEG, EOG, chin EMG, and leg EMG
Tiered acquisition model with escalation nights deploying full PSG after technician-flagged events
review: Controls sensor suite configuration vs. participant safety. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on participant comfort and willingness to participate.

name: Data Sharing Protocol
consequences: Immediate: Increased data sharing and collaboration → Systemic: Enhanced research productivity and knowledge dissemination → Strategic: Improved research outcomes and grant prospects.
options: Public data sharing with de-identified physiological and sensor data
Limited public data sharing with restricted access to bedroom video
Private data sharing with only the research team having access
review: Controls data sharing protocol vs. participant privacy. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on participant trust and willingness to participate.

name: Event-Triage Algorithm Development
consequences: Immediate: Improved event-triage efficiency → Systemic: Enhanced data analysis and research outcomes → Strategic: Increased research opportunities and publication prospects.
options: Development of semi-automated event-triage tools that rank probable parasomnia episodes
Implementation of a fully automated event-triage system
Use of machine learning algorithms to improve event-triage accuracy
review: Controls event-triage algorithm development vs. research validity. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on participant safety and data quality.

name: Governance Structure
consequences: Immediate: Improved governance and oversight → Systemic: Enhanced accountability and research integrity → Strategic: Increased research opportunities and publication prospects.
options: 3-member external scientific advisory board reviewing progress every 6 months
Regular meetings with the PI and core team to discuss progress and challenges
Establishment of a formal data safety monitoring board
review: Controls governance structure vs. research ethics. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on participant involvement and feedback.

name: Neurological Screening Protocol
consequences: Immediate: Improved participant safety → Systemic: Enhanced data quality and research validity → Strategic: Increased research opportunities and publication prospects.
options: Screening for nocturnal epilepsy and other neurological conditions before admission
Conducting neurological screening during the residential monitoring period
Implementing a risk-based approach to neurological screening
review: Controls neurological screening protocol vs. participant safety. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on participant anxiety and stress levels.

name: Staffing Model Optimization
consequences: Immediate: Improved staffing efficiency → Systemic: Enhanced data collection and research outcomes → Strategic: Increased research opportunities and publication prospects.
options: Rotating night shifts with 3 research technicians per shift
Hiring additional staff to cover more participants and events
Implementing a flexible staffing model that adapts to changing participant needs
review: Controls staffing model optimization vs. participant care. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on staff burnout and turnover.

name: Participant Compensation Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Improved participant engagement → Systemic: Enhanced data quality and research outcomes → Strategic: Increased research opportunities and publication prospects.
options: Per-night compensation of €80 plus meals
Offering additional incentives for participants with rare parasomnia events
Implementing a tiered compensation system based on participant participation levels
review: Controls participant compensation strategy vs. participant motivation. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on participant financial stability and well-being.

name: Facility Expansion Plan
consequences: Immediate: Increased research capacity → Systemic: Enhanced data collection and research outcomes → Strategic: Increased research opportunities and publication prospects.
options: Phased expansion of the facility to accommodate more participants and events
Implementing a modular design for easy expansion and upgradeability
Establishing a satellite facility in a different location
review: Controls facility expansion plan vs. participant safety and comfort. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on staff training and readiness for expanded operations.

name: Data Retention Policy
consequences: Immediate: Improved data security → Systemic: Enhanced research integrity and accountability → Strategic: Increased research opportunities and publication prospects.
options: Storing data in a secure, off-site location with regular backups
Implementing a tiered data retention policy based on participant consent levels
Establishing a data archiving system for long-term preservation
review: Controls data retention policy vs. participant privacy. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on research reproducibility and transparency.

name: Sleep Clinic Partnership Model
consequences: Immediate: Improved participant access → Systemic: Enhanced data quality and research outcomes → Strategic: Increased research opportunities and publication prospects.
options: Exclusive partnership with the University Hospital Bonn sleep clinic
Collaboration with multiple sleep clinics in the region
Establishing a network of sleep clinics across Germany
review: Controls sleep clinic partnership model vs. participant recruitment. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on participant diversity and representation.

name: Participant Engagement Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Improved participant engagement → Systemic: Enhanced data quality and research outcomes → Strategic: Increased research opportunities and publication prospects.
options: Providing educational materials and resources for participants
Offering support groups and counseling services for participants
Implementing a participant feedback system to improve the research experience
review: Controls participant engagement strategy vs. participant satisfaction. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on staff workload and resource allocation.

name: Equipment Maintenance Schedule
consequences: Immediate: Improved equipment reliability → Systemic: Enhanced data quality and research outcomes → Strategic: Increased research opportunities and publication prospects.
options: Regular maintenance schedule with scheduled downtime for repairs
On-demand maintenance based on equipment performance metrics
Implementing a predictive maintenance system to prevent equipment failures
review: Controls equipment maintenance schedule vs. participant safety. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on staff training and readiness for equipment operation.

name: Research Publication Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Improved research visibility → Systemic: Enhanced research reputation and credibility → Strategic: Increased research opportunities and publication prospects.
options: Publishing research findings in high-impact factor journals
Presenting research at international conferences and workshops
Establishing a research blog or podcast to share updates and insights
review: Controls research publication strategy vs. research impact. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on participant representation and involvement.

name: Participant Retention Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Improved participant retention → Systemic: Enhanced data quality and research outcomes → Strategic: Increased research opportunities and publication prospects.
options: Providing incentives for participants to continue their involvement in the research study
Offering support services to help participants manage their parasomnia events
Implementing a tiered participant retention system based on participation levels
review: Controls participant retention strategy vs. participant satisfaction. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on staff workload and resource allocation.
